level_signals: Central / strategic role ('center of Sierra's product')

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 3+ years of product management experience, ideally working on developer platforms, AI systems, or complex technical products
must_haves.qualifier: ideally in developer platforms, AI systems, or complex technical products

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Strong technical depth - Ability to engage deeply with engineers on system design (e.g., simulation systems, evaluation frameworks, APIs, and platform architecture)
Experience building 0→1 products or platforms - Comfortable defining new abstractions and workflows in ambiguous spaces
Experience working with AI systems - Familiarity with LLMs and the challenges of building, testing, and iterating on non-deterministic systems
Strong product instincts for builder tools - You have taste in developer experience and can balance power, flexibility, and usability

scope.surface: Agent Studio — the core environment where agents are created, tested, and improved; includes journeys, chat, simulations, workspaces, packages, and AI copilots (Ghostwriter and Explorer)
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS platform for enterprise AI agent development
primary_metrics_inferred: Speed of build → test → release loop
Simulation coverage and failure detection rates
core_responsibilities: Define the agent building experience across journeys, chat, and workflows
Own the Agent Development Life Cycle (Analyze → Build → Test → Release)
Build simulation and testing systems for agent validation
Design reusable systems through packages for integrations, skills, and behaviors
Integrate AI copilots (Ghostwriter and Explorer) into the Agent Studio workflow
Make agent quality measurable and actionable through evaluation frameworks and feedback systems

compensation.equity_form: Eligible full-time employees can participate in Sierra's equity plans subject to the terms of the applicable plans and policies

green_flags: Clear product scope and ownership area (Agent Studio with named sub-systems)
High-profile co-founders with strong AI and product pedigree (Bret Taylor, Clay Bavor)
Central strategic role described as 'the center of Sierra's product'
Encourages applications even if experience doesn't precisely match
0→1 + scaling combination signals meaningful product ownership
red_flags: Primarily in-person San Francisco role — limited flexibility for remote candidates
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed
Minimum years requirement (3+) is moderate but role complexity implies senior expectations not fully reflected in the bar stated

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years_required_min and role centrality, not explicitly stated)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and customer focus)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from 'primarily an in-person company based in San Francisco')
compensation.source set to 'unknown' as no figures provided
ambiguities: The JD mentions 'ideally working on developer platforms, AI systems, or complex technical products' alongside the 3+ years requirement — it is unclear whether this domain context is a hard requirement or a soft preference. Treated as a qualifier on a hard requirement given the overall role demands.
No explicit mention of visa sponsorship or work authorization eligibility anywhere in the JD.
Nice-to-haves section is absent; all listed qualifications are treated as must-haves given they appear in 'What you'll bring' without 'preferred' or 'bonus' language.
